response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced senior-to-staff engineer with 15+ years of professional development, making him severely overqualified for this entry-level position. While he demonstrates strong OOP fundamentals, agile practices, and a relevant CS degree, his Java exposure is secondary to TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Most critically, his German is self-assessed as 'Basic,' which falls well short of the strong written and verbal German required for client-facing consulting work in the German insurance sector.
strengths: Solid OOP and software craftsmanship foundation including TDD, CI/CD, and agile methodologies across many projects
Java listed in skills with hands-on usage at Altom Consulting and National Instruments; CS degree from University of Bucharest satisfies educational requirement
Based in Berlin with a long history of collaborative, client-facing, and cross-functional project work
concerns: Severely overqualified — Staff/Senior engineer applying for an entry-level role presents a serious retention and motivation risk
German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic,' which is incompatible with the role's requirement for strong verbal and written German in client-facing insurance consulting
Java is a peripheral skill; primary expertise centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang with no visible insurance-sector experience
